summary: sensivity ships obfuscated payload with login persistence, host fingerprinting, and bundled game-cheat native module
details: On require() of this package, launcher.js sets process.title to 'Runtime Broker' to impersonate a legitimate Windows broker process, then writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run registry entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points to wscript.exe running the bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs. The VBS script silently re-launches `node.exe launcher.js` with a hidden window (objShell.Run..., 0, False) at every user login, establishing persistence under a name that impersonates Microsoft OneDrive. launcher.js then eval()s the contents of server.obf.js, an 81 KB RC4 + base64 obfuscator.io bundle (397-entry rotated string array with inline RC4 decoder). The deobfuscated server reads the Windows MachineGuid from HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography, the OS hostname, and the C: volume serial number (via `cmd /c vol` / Get-Volume), hashes them into an HWID, and POSTs {key, hwid, nonce, app, version} JSON to a hardcoded license endpoint baked into the obfuscated bundle. The package also ships sens.node, a 6.8 MB Windows PE native addon loaded as `forte`, whose strings ('Teleport', 'LeftHand', 'LeftFoot', 'Head', per-bone offsets) identify it as game-process memory manipulation tooling (aimbot/ESP) — contradicting the package's 'Mobile Control Panel' self-description. A Socket.IO API exposes start/stop/setConfig to drive the cheat module from a phone over a QR-code-shared LAN URL. cli.js's `update` subcommand additionally runs `taskkill /F /IM node.exe` and `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe` (killing every Node and PowerShell process on the host, not just this package's), then `npm i -g sensivity@latest` — an unpinned self-update channel that lets the author push arbitrary future code to anyone who runs `sens update`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
